name: Malleshwaram Bakery Walk
description:
What can a bakery reveal about a city?

Join us for a guided walk through some of Bengaluru’s neighbourhood bakeries as we trace the histories, communities, and everyday food cultures that shaped them. Beginning in the heart of Malleshwaram, the walk moves through bakeries known for warm khara buns, sweet dilpasands, plain bread cake and more.

name: Gulistan (The Land of Flowers) and Other Histories
description:
How do plants travel across cultures, histories, and oceans?

Join Gopa Trivedi, artist of Gulistan, which maps a garden of ingredients that travelled far before landing on Indian soil—seeds carried by trade, colonial routes, and ocean currents. In this talk, she shares the explorations, thoughts, questions, and journeys that shape and inform her visual arts practice: through images, ideas, and encounters that have accumulated over a decade of working at the intersection of the personal, the historical, and the deeply uncertain.

name: The Language of Materials - Understanding Art Through Science in CALORIE
description:
What happens when we look at artworks through the lens of material science? From palm oil and sugar crystals to plastics and DNA. The materials artists choose, shapes not only how an artwork looks and feels but also how they give life to ideas.

Artists and Scientists ask similar questions about materials:, How do they change? What do they reveal? And what are they capable of becoming? 

Through food and a diverse range of natural, synthetic, and biological materials, we invite you to look closely and explore the language of materials.

Join us as we uncover how the understanding of material science can open new ways of seeing art. Together, we will explore how materials react, transform and communicate – revealing stories, processes and possibilities embedded within them.

name: Forbidden Desire: Sugar Blowing Workshop
description:
Sugar is a material full of contrasts, a sweet delight and a forbidden indulgence. Inspired by artist Jade Armstrong’s original concept, this workshop explores the delicate balance between beauty, sweetness, and the fleeting nature of life.

In this workshop, Chef Pratyay will guide the participants to learn and to create intricate sugar-blown sculptures, each capturing a single breath and symbolising both the passage of time and the fragility of life. As these sculptures slowly melt into syrupy residue, they reflect sugar’s transformation from a prized luxury to an overconsumed treat.

This hands-on experience encourages reflection on desire, decay, and consumption.

name: Eat, Read, Think
description:
Eat, Read, Think is a reading circle run by Science Gallery Bengaluru, bringing together curious minds to explore ideas through literature and conversation. Readings will be themed around the current exhibition season, CALORIE, exploring humanity's fraught and intimate relationship with food through readings inspired by the many exhibits present throughout the gallery. Eat, Read, Think welcomes young people from all walks of life to gather for conversations that connect science, culture and society.

The ninth edition of the circle and final of this exhibition season will read excerpts from 'Taste' by Giorgio Agamben.

Come for community stay for snacks!

name: Food that Climbed the Food Chain and Controlled it
description:
We are talking about humans!

Two million years ago, the early humans jumped the food chain to become the biggest predators of the land. We hunted animals in large numbers driving the biggest mammals on the planet to extinction. But, 12000 years ago, something changed. We exited from the food chain and started growing our food, creating an all new food chain for ourselves. 
From being food ourselves to completely controlling our food ecosystem, we have come a long way! Ever wondered what our journey must have been like to become the versions we are today? 

Join Harsha Jagadeesh, as she explores our journey as species and everything we created and destroyed to make today happen.

name: The Endurance Equation: How Humans Outrun Hunger
description:
What if every calorie we consume carries the weight of human history, geopolitical warfare, and the hidden limits of our own biology?

From the violent rewriting of agrarian landscapes in Southeast Asia to the deep physiological footprints left inside our gut microbiomes by historical famines, food has always been a battleground. Join our CALORIE Mediators, Supriya and Kritiman  as they explore how historical displacements, colonial control, and corporate food systems alter both entire ecosystems and our literal cellular memory, in The Endurance Equation : How Humans Outrun Hunger.

name: Whose Waste Is It Anyway?
description:
When we hear the word “waste”, we often think of the things we throw away. But waste is much more than a bin full of discarded objects. It can be food that never gets eaten, resources used carelessly, effort that is overlooked, pollutants that enter our bodies, or materials that one system rejects and another depends on.

This walkthrough invites us to look at waste not as an endpoint, but as part of a larger cycle. As we move through the exhibition, we will ask where waste comes from and who lives with its consequences. 

Join our Experimentor, Sai, as we follow the many lives of waste. By the end, we may find that waste tells us as much about what we value as it does about what we discard.

name: The Rat Race! Entangled Appetites in CALORIE
description: Pests, harbingers of plague, indicators of famines - Rodents, often thought to be at the bottom of the food chain, are ever present entities in our food systems. 'The Rat Race! Entangled Appetites in CALORIE' explores the various roles of rodents in CALORIE. Join Aashka, Megha, Tejashree and Vinaya to discuss the lives and deeds of our small friends across farms, factories, cities and plates.

name: The Ghosts of Time
description: For our upcoming exhibition season QUANTUM, Lyndsey Walsh is exhibiting Fowl Actions at a Distance, a multimedia installation that explores quantum navigation and entanglement through trained chickens. In this workshop, participants will observe Lyndsey’s ongoing experiment to train chickens to detect electromagnetic anomalies. Together, we will explore how ideas of time and space are constructed through culture, politics, and technology. Participants will be invited to consider alternative ways of measuring, perceiving, and experiencing time. The workshop will also introduce the idea of non-human experiences of time, encouraging participants to reflect on how different bodies, beings, and cultures navigate the world.

name: Green Revolution: Reflections from the Lab, Field and Archive
description: A scientist, a development commissioner, and an archivist come together to reflect on the past, present, and future of agriculture and agricultural science in India. Beginning with M. S. Swaminathan’s life and his vision for an “evergreen revolution,” the conversation will trace the ruptures and continuities in Indian agriculture from the 1960s to the present. The discussion will explore the evolution of genetics and agricultural science, the lived realities between the laboratory and the field, and the ways in which histories of agriculture are written, preserved, and remembered.
